subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Evaporating Atmospheres
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Evaporating Atmospheres.

hook.text: Some planets are losing their atmospheres. Intense radiation from their stars strips gas away into space.

buildup.text: Hot Neptunes close to their stars are especially vulnerable. Ultraviolet light heats the upper atmosphere until it escapes.

discovery.text: GJ 3470b is losing its atmosphere at a rate of 10 billion grams per second—it's literally evaporating.

twist.text: This explains the 'hot Neptune desert'—a gap where few Neptune-sized planets exist very close to their stars.

climax.text: Atmospheric escape may transform mini-Neptunes into bare rocky super-Earths over billions of years.

punchline.text: Stars can boil planets down to their cores. Neptune today, super-Earth tomorrow.

quiz.question: What is the 'hot Neptune desert'?
options: A gap where few Neptune-sized planets exist close to their stars, likely due to atmospheric stripping
A dry region on Neptune-like planets caused by solar radiation
An area in the galaxy where no Neptune-mass planets have been found
